questions.question: In the leap year 2024, a sports academy conducted a trial on 28 February, which was a Wednesday. The final selection was conducted on 12 March 2024. On which day of the week was the final selection conducted?
questions.explanation: 28 Feb 2024 is Wednesday. 2024 is a leap year, so February has 29 days. From 28 Feb to 29 Feb = 1 day. From 29 Feb to 12 Mar = 12 days. Total shift = 1 + 12 = 13 days. 13 days = 7 + 6 days, so the day advances by 6 days. Wednesday + 6 days = Tuesday. Answer: Tuesday

questions.question: In each question below is given a statement followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. You have to assume everything in the statement to be true and on the basis of the information given in the statement, decide which of the suggested courses of action logically follow(s) for pursuing. Statement: Some customers complain of stomach discomfort after eating bread from a few local shops, but no official food-test report has yet confirmed contamination or adulteration. Course of action I: Arrest all bakery owners in the district immediately and seal their shops permanently without any testing. Course of action II: Ban the sale of bread in the entire market for the next six months.
options: Only course of action I follows.
Neither course of action I nor II follows.
Only course of action II follows.
Both course of action I and II follow.
questions.explanation: Course of action I is not justified because punishment without testing or confirmation is unfair and does not establish the real cause. Course of action II is also excessive because a blanket ban without confirmed evidence harms public access and honest sellers. Therefore, neither course of action I nor II follows.

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'A + B' means 'A is the mother of B' 'A - B' means 'A is the father of B' 'A $ B' means 'A is the son of B' 'A % B' means 'A is the sister of B' 'A & B' means 'A is the wife of B' 'A ÷ B' means 'A is the brother of B' Which of the following coded chains means K is the father-in-law of O?
questions.explanation: Option (a) K ÷ V % W + X $ O K ÷ V → K is brother of V V % W → V is sister of W => W is sibling of V W + X → W is mother of X => X is child of W X $ O → X is son of O => O is parent of X ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28180/image037.jpg) => K is the brother-in-law of O. Option (b) K + L - M $ N % O K + L → K is mother of L L - M → L is father of M => M is grandchild of K M $ N → M is son of N => M is child of N N % O → N is sister of O => O is sibling of N ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28180/image038.jpg) => K is the mother-in-law of O's sister. Option (c) K $ S ÷ T & U - O K $ S → K is son of S S ÷ T → S is brother of T => T is sibling of S T & U → T is wife of U => U is husband of T U - O → U is father of O => O is child of U ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28180/image039.jpg) => K is the cousin of O. Option (d) K - P + Q % R $ O K - P → K is father of P P + Q → P is mother of Q => Q is child of P Q % R → Q is sister of R => R is child of P R $ O → R is son of O => O is parent of R ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28180/image040.jpg) => K is the father-in-law of O.

questions.question: The policy of ring-fence was associated with _______.
questions.explanation: The "Policy of Ring-Fence" is primarily associated with Warren Hastings, who was the Governor-General of Bengal from 1773 to 1785. This policy, followed roughly from 1765 to 1813, aimed at creating buffer zones to protect the British East India Company's territories from external threats. The Company would defend the frontiers of its neighboring states on the condition that these states would bear the expenses of the defending army. The main objective was to safeguard the Company's own possessions by forming a defensive ring around them. Explanation of Other Options: (b) Lord Mayo: Governor-General and Viceroy of India (1869-1872), known for financial decentralization and establishing statistical surveys. (c) Lord Dalhousie: Governor-General of India (1848-1856), known for the Doctrine of Lapse, introduction of railways, telegraph, and postal reforms. (d) Lord Wellesley: Governor-General of India (1798-1805), known for the policy of Subsidiary Alliance, which was an extension and evolution of defensive alliances but more focused on bringing Indian states under British paramountcy. Further insights: The Policy of Ring-Fence was essentially a defensive strategy where the Company was more concerned with protecting its own borders by ensuring the security of its neighbors. This policy gradually evolved into more assertive policies like the Subsidiary Alliance under Wellesley, which aimed at greater control over Indian states.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the Tianjin Declaration as adopted during the 25th SCO Council of Heads of State summit?
options: An agreement signed in Tianjin to establish a common trade currency and remove tariffs among SCO countries.
To promote regional security cooperation, counter-terrorism, AI governance, and economic collaboration among member states.
A defence treaty signed in Tianjin to create a joint military force among SCO member states.
An environmental agreement adopted in Tianjin to reduce industrial pollution and address climate change in Asia.
questions.explanation: The Tianjin Declaration, adopted at the 25th SCO Council of Heads of State summit in September 2025 in Tianjin, China, established a comprehensive framework for regional security, technological, and economic cooperation. It intensified efforts against terrorism-specifically citing regional attacks-promoted an AI governance framework, and reinforced a multipolar world order through the SCO's "Shanghai Spirit" Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited China for the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation summit in Tianjin. The Shanghai Cooperation Organisation Summit 2025 brings together major Eurasian leaders in Tianjin, China, with regional security, trade, and geopolitical alignments topping the agenda. About the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO). Founded: 2001 in Shanghai. Members: 10 (China, Russia, India, Pakistan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Uzbekistan, Iran, Belarus). Mandate: Regional security, counter-terrorism, economic cooperation, connectivity, cultural and technological exchanges. Key Agreements & Documents Adopted Tianjin Declaration and SCO Development Strategy (2026-2035) were formally adopted, outlining a political, security, economic, and technological roadmap for the next decade. A Statement commemorating the 80th anniversary of both the end of World War II and the UN's founding was issued, affirming shared values like multilateralism and peaceful cooperation. Over 20 documents were signed, covering key areas such as AI, green industry, sustainable energy, scientific collaboration, multilateral trade, and security cooperation.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are not correct? a. The Supreme Court can issue the writs under Article 226. b. Only the Supreme Court and the high courts can issue the writs and not any other court. c. The writ jurisdiction of the High Court is narrower than that of Supreme Court. d. The High court can issue writs only for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights. Answer Options:
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court (under Article 32) and the high courts (under Article 226) can issue the writs of habeas corpus, mandamus, prohibition, certiorari and quo-warranto. - Only the Supreme Court and the high courts can issue the writs and not any other court. - The Supreme Court can issue writs only for the enforcement of fundamental rights whereas a high court can issue writs not only for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights but also for any other purpose. - The expression 'for any other purpose' refers to the enforcement of an ordinary legal right. - Thus, the writ jurisdiction of the Supreme Court, in this respect, is narrower than that of high court.

questions.question: Consider the following two statements, labeled Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Evaluate them and choose the appropriate answer from the given options. Assertion (A): The Khelo India scheme has significantly broadened the talent pool for various sports at the grassroots level in India. Reason (R): The primary objective of the scheme is to identify and nurture young sporting talent from a very early age through scholarships and state-of-the-art infrastructure.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
questions.explanation: Both the Assertion and the Reason are correct, and the Reason provides the direct explanation for the Assertion. Assertion (A) is true: The Khelo India Games (including University, Youth, and Winter editions) have become a major national platform, unearthing talented athletes from all corners of the country who might have otherwise gone unnoticed. This has demonstrably widened the base of athletes available for selection to national camps. Reason (R) is true: The stated mission of the Khelo India scheme is to build a strong framework for all sports played in our country and establish India as a great sporting nation. This is achieved by identifying talent, providing annual financial assistance of ₹5 lakh per athlete for 8 years, and developing sports infrastructure. Relationship: The reason the talent pool has broadened (Assertion) is precisely because the scheme is designed to identify and nurture that talent (Reason). Therefore, R is the correct explanation of A.

questions.question: Identify the incorrect statement. I. According to the Census 2011, Thane district in Maharashtra had the highest population among all districts in India. II. According to the Census of India 2011, Champhai district in Mizoram has the highest literacy rate in India.
questions.explanation: According to the Census 2011, Thane district in Maharashtra had the highest population among all districts in India, with a population of 11 million (1.1 crore). According to the Census of India 2011, Serchhip district in Mizoram has the highest literacy rate in India at 97.91%. This small district outperformed others due to strong educational initiatives in the state of Mizoram, which overall has high literacy. "Champhai", also in Mizoram, has a literacy rate of 95.91%, lower than Serchhip.

questions.question: Fundamental Rights -
options: Can be suspended by the order of Prime Minister
May be suspended on the will of President
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India guarantees Fundamental Rights, but they are not absolute. They can be suspended under specific circumstances, most notably during the proclamation of a National Emergency. When a National Emergency is declared under Article 352 of the Constitution (on grounds of war, external aggression, or armed rebellion), the enforcement of most Fundamental Rights can be suspended. Article 358 automatically suspends the six rights under Article 19 (like freedom of speech, assembly, etc.) for the duration of the emergency (if declared on grounds of war or external aggression). Article 359 authorizes the President to issue an order to suspend the right of citizens to move to court for the enforcement of other Fundamental Rights. Crucial Exception: The rights guaranteed under Article 20 (protection in respect of conviction for offenses) and Article 21 (protection of life and personal liberty) can NEVER be suspended, even during an emergency. This protection was added by the 44th Amendment Act, 1978. Explanation of Other Options: a) Cannot be suspended: This is incorrect. As explained above, the Constitution provides for their suspension during an emergency. b) Can be suspended by the order of Prime Minister: This is incorrect. The Prime Minister and the Council of Ministers advise the President to declare an emergency, but the formal proclamation and the order to suspend rights are issued by the President. c) May be suspended on the will of President: This is misleading and incorrect. The President cannot suspend Fundamental Rights based on personal "will" or discretion. This action can only be taken on the advice of the cabinet and strictly according to the procedures laid down in the Constitution for an emergency.

questions.question: Choose the correct statement(s) regarding the approval of "Money bill". (I) Money bill can be introduced only in Lok Sabha and not in Rajya Sabha and that too with the prior approval of and on behalf of the President. (II) After being passed by the Lok Sabha, the bill goes to the Rajya Sabha. Rajya Sabha has 14 days at its disposal for consideration and report. (III) The Rajya Sabha can reject the money bill if they feel the bill doesn't have enough content to proceed.
questions.explanation: The money bills are such bills which deal with money matters like imposition of taxes, governmental expenditure, and borrowings etc. In case there is a dispute as to whether a bill is a money bill or not, the Speaker's decision is final. The money bill has to undergo three readings like an ordinary bill, but a few considerations are also added here. They are: - (i) Money bill can be introduced only in Lok Sabha and not in Rajya Sabha and that too with the prior approval of and on behalf of the President. (ii) After being passed by the Lok Sabha, the bill goes to the Rajya Sabha. Rajya Sabha has 14 days at its disposal for consideration and report. (iii) The Rajya Sabha cannot reject the money bill. It may either accept it or make recommendations. (iv) In case Rajya Sabha chooses to make recommendations, the bill will return to Lok Sabha. The Lok Sabha may accept these recommendations or reject them. In any case the bill will not go back to Rajya Sabha. Instead, it will be sent directly to the President for his assent. (v) If the Rajya Sabha does not return the bill within 14 days, it will be deemed to have been passed by both the Houses of the Parliament and sent to the President for his assent.

questions.question: Revenue Deficit as a percentage of Fiscal Deficit is calculated by the formula _____.
questions.explanation: · Option A is correct because the ratio is (Revenue Deficit / Fiscal Deficit) X 100 to express the share of revenue gap in total fiscal gap. · Revenue Deficit = Revenue Expenditure - Revenue Receipts; Fiscal Deficit = Total Expenditure - Total Receipts (excluding borrowings). · This percentage indicates how much of government borrowing covers purely revenue shortfall. · Option B is incorrect as it inverts the ratio. · Option C is incorrect because it uses Total Expenditure instead of Fiscal Deficit. · Option D is incorrect because it computes revenue spending efficiency, not deficit ratio.

questions.question: Which festival is celebrated by the Khasis of Meghalaya to mark the completion of harvest?
questions.explanation: The festival celebrated by the Khasis of Meghalaya to mark the completion of harvest is the Nongkrem Dance Festival. Shad Nongkrem is the most important festival of the Khasi tribe from Meghalaya. It is a five day festival held in beginning November to offer thanksgiving for a good harvest. The Nongkrem dance festival is performed to appease the all powerful Goddess Ka Blei Synshar for a rich bumper harvest and prosperity of the people. Other options Lai Haraoba is a festival of Manipur, not Meghalaya Wangala is the harvest festival of the Garo tribe (not Khasi), also from Meghalaya Kharchi Puja is a festival celebrated in Tripura

Tributaries of other rivers: Godavari: Manjra, Penganga, Wardha, Wainganga, Indravati, Sabari, Pranhita. Narmada: Its principal tributaries are the Burhner, the Halon, the Heran, the Banjar, the Dudhi, the Shakkar, the Tawa, the Barna ,the Kolar, the Ganjal, the Beda, the Goi, and the Orsang. Kaveri: Arkavathy, Shimsha, Hemavati, Kapila, Shimsha, Honnuhole, Amaravati, Lakshmana Kabini, Lokapavani, Bhavani, Noyyal, and Tirtha.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true about Chandrayaan-4? A. Chandrayaan-4 aims to bring back moon rocks and soil (regolith) to Earth. B. This mission will involve the use of two separate rockets. C. Chandrayaan-4 will feature five spacecraft modules.
questions.explanation: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is set to begin its next lunar mission, Chandrayaan-4, aiming to bring back moon rocks and soil (regolith) to Earth. This mission will involve the use of two separate rockets - the heavy-lifter LVM-3 and ISRO's reliable workhorse PSLV - to carry distinct payloads for the same lunar mission. Scheduled for launch no earlier than 2028, Chandrayaan-4 will mark India's entry into the select group of nations with the capability to collect samples from the lunar surface and safely return them to Earth for scientific studies. According to ISRO Chairman S Somanath's recent presentation at the National Space Science Symposium, Chandrayaan-4 will feature five spacecraft modules: 1.Propulsion Module 2.Descender Module 3.Ascender Module 4.Transfer Module 5.Re-entry Module

questions.question: Who became only the second batter in Ranji Trophy history to cross the 10,000-run mark during the 2025-26 Ranji Trophy second semi-final played at Kalyani in 2026?
questions.explanation: Jammu and Kashmir skipper Paras Dogra became the second player to cross the 10,000-run mark in the history of Ranji Trophy. The 41-year-old achieved the feat during the ongoing second semi-final of the 2025-26 Ranji Trophy season being played between Jammu and Kashmir and Bengal in Kalyani. Dogra's 10,000th run in India's premier First Class competition came when he completed the 11th run of his innings. At the time of writing, Dogra was unbeaten on 55 off 105 balls with seven fours to his name. Dogra made his First Class debut during the 2001/02 season. He represented the state of his birth Himachal Pradesh for over a decade. In 2024, he shifted base and started playing for Jammu and Kashmir. Soon after moving to J&K, he was made the state team's captain. The right-handed batter has played 151 First Class games, amassing over 10,000 runs and has averaged 48.15. This includes 34 hundreds and 35 fifties. His highest score at this level is 253. The first player to achieve the feat was former Indian Test opener Wasim Jaffer. Dogra is the only current player in the top 10 Ranji Trophy run scorers' list.

questions.question: ____________ is a body of water-saturated sediment or rock in which water can move readily.
questions.explanation: Groundwater can be found in aquifers. An aquifer is a body of water-saturated sediment or rock in which water can move readily. There are two main types of aquifers: unconfined and confined. An unconfined aquifer is a partially or fully filled aquifer that is exposed to the surface of the land. Because this aquifer is in contact with the atmosphere, it is impacted by meteoric water and any kind of surface contamination. If the water is pressurized at a high enough value, when a well is drilled into the confining aquifer, water rises above the surface of the ground.

questions.question: What was the total cheetah population in the country as of March 2026?
questions.explanation: Union Minister for Environment, Forest and Climate Change Shri Bhupender Yadav informed that Jwala, a Namibian cheetah and a successful third-time mother, gave birth to five cubs at Kuno National Park. The number of Indian-born thriving cubs had risen to 33, marking the 10th successful cheetah litter on Indian soil. He added that with the arrival of these cubs, the total Cheetah population in India had reached 53. Recently, Gamini, a South African Cheetah, became a mother for the second time, giving birth to four cubs. Further, on February 28, 2026, Shri Yadav, released nine cheetahs ' 6 females and 3 males ' received from Botswana into quarantine enclosures at Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh. The animals are undergoing an acclimatisation and health monitoring phase before their gradual release into the larger landscape.

questions.question: The type of unemployment that is widely noticed in Indian Agriculture is
questions.explanation: Disguised unemployment (also known as hidden unemployment) is a type of unemployment that is widely prevalent in the agricultural sector of developing countries like India. It occurs when more people are employed in an activity than are actually needed, so the marginal productivity of some workers is zero or very low. If some of these workers were removed, the total output would not decrease significantly. Explanation of Other Options: Frictional unemployment: This is temporary unemployment that occurs when people are in the process of changing jobs or entering the workforce for the first time. Structural unemployment: This arises from a mismatch between the skills of the workforce and the skills demanded by employers, often due to technological changes or shifts in the structure of the economy. Cyclical unemployment: This is unemployment that results from fluctuations in the business cycle (e.g., during economic recessions or downturns). Further insights: In Indian agriculture, particularly in family-based farming, often all members of the family are engaged on the farm, even if the work could be done by fewer people. This surplus labor contributes to disguised unemployment. Seasonal unemployment is also common in agriculture, where work is available only during certain seasons (e.g., sowing and harvesting). Addressing disguised unemployment often involves creating alternative employment opportunities in non-agricultural sectors and improving agricultural productivity.

questions.question: The Kalbeliya dance, recognized by UNESCO, is traditionally performed by which Kalbelia group in the state of _______.
questions.explanation: • Kalbeliya dance, performed by the Kalbelia tribe in Rajasthan, is recognized as an Intangible Cultural Heritage by UNESCO. It is characterized by the snake-like movements of the dancers. • Bhil: Introduced the Ghoomar dance, later adopted by Rajasthani royalty. • Meena: Participate in Bhavai, a balancing dance involving brass pitchers. • Gujjar: Known for performing Chari dance in the Kishangarh region. Further Insight: • Rajasthan ○ Ghoomar - Performed by women, originally a Bhil tribe dance ○ Jhulan Leela ○ Chari - Symbolizes joy and ritual of collecting water in pots, performed by Saini and Gujjar communities ○ Ghapal ○ Suisini ○ Ganagor ○ Jhuma ○ Bhavai - Performed by Jat, Meena, Bhil, and Kalbelia tribes ○ Panihari - Associated with women fetching water ○ Kalbeliya - Performed by Kalbeliya tribe, UNESCO intangible cultural heritage

questions.question: Crocodiles have a
questions.explanation: Crocodiles, along with other crocodilians like alligators and caimans, are unique among living reptiles as they possess a complete 4-chambered heart. This heart consists of two atria and two fully separated ventricles, a feature they share with birds and mammals. This advanced cardiac structure allows for the complete separation of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood, ensuring a highly efficient circulatory system. Explanation of Other Options: 3-chambered heart: Found in most other reptiles (lizards, snakes, turtles) and amphibians. It has two atria and one ventricle, where some mixing of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood occurs. 2-chambered heart: Characteristic of fish, consisting of a single atrium and a single ventricle. single chambered heart: No vertebrate possesses a single-chambered heart. Further insights: Despite having a four-chambered heart, crocodiles have a unique anatomical feature called the Foramen of Panizza. This is a small opening that connects the left and right aortic arches, allowing them to shunt blood away from their lungs while submerged, which helps conserve oxygen during long dives.

Now, third day's work done = 5 = 5 units Fourth day's work done = -4 = -4 units Then, four days' work done = 1 + 5 - 4 = 2 units This means 1 unit work done in 2 days, 2 units work done in 4 days and so on. So, (2 * 55 = 110) 110 days' work done = 1 * 55 = 55 units Remaining work = 60 - 55 = 5 units Therefore, 5 units work will be done by P on 111th day. questions.question: Which of the following sentence is grammatically and contextually correct?
options: Amazon took steps unprecedented at the company to offers leniency.
It was up to them to choose what to discuss in their diary.
They looked like a lavender strings of pearls glowing in the night sky.
I just couldn't control or understand what was happening to I.
questions.explanation: Sentence (b) is correct. Sentence (a) is incorrect because the infinitive marker 'to' is always followed by the base verb so 'offer' would be apt in place of 'offers'. Sentence (c) is incorrect because plural noun 'strings' need to be replaced with singular noun 'string' as article 'a' refers to it which is used for singular nouns only. Sentence (d) is incorrect because personal pronoun 'I' is used as the subject of the sentence, not object so it needs to be replaced with objective pronoun 'me'. Hence, option (b) is correct. Infinitive: The infinitive is the base form of a verb, often preceded by to. Consider the sentences given below: · To err is human. [like a noun, the infinitive 'to err' is the subject of the verb 'is'] · My father likes to cook. [like a noun, the infinitive 'to cook' is the object of the verb 'likes'] · To vote in elections is our right. [the infinitive 'to vote', like a noun, is the subject of the verb 'is', but, like a verb, it also takes an object (elections)] · Radha went to the lake to fetch water. [the infinitive 'to fetch', like a noun, is the object of the verb 'went', but, like a verb, it also takes an object (water)] · The above sentences illustrate various ways in which infinitives are used. It can both act as a noun and a verb. Objective Pronouns: The pronouns me, you, us, him, her, it, and them are called objective pronouns because they act as the objects of verbs and prepositions: Anjali drove us home.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one word substitute for the given group of words. The repetition of usually initial consonant sounds in two or more neighbouring words.

For example, the word "apple" has two syllables: "ap" and "ple." Malapropism: The mistaken use of a word in place of a similar-sounding one, often with unintentionally humorous results. Recitation: The act of reciting something from memory, often aloud, such as a poem or speech. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The ropes were holding the tent the whole night. The winds were disrupting the power supply.
options: The tent was being held by the ropes the whole night. The power supply was disrupted by the winds.
The tent was held by the ropes the whole night. The power supply was being disrupted by the winds.
The tent was being held by the ropes the whole night. The power supply was being disrupted by the winds.
The tent being held by the ropes the whole night. The power supply being disrupted by the winds.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of the verb ['being' here] is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in Past Continuous. Thus, the Passive voice will also be in Past Continuous. The format of Active voice is: Subject + was/were + V1 + ing + Object. Thus, in Passive voice, the format will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + was/were + being + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case). For example: Amit was practising Math. (Active Voice) Math was being practised by Amit. (Passive Voice) The options except (c) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Hence, the correct answer is option (c). Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. These technologies create images depicting people in compromising situations, posing significant ethical and security concerns about truth and authenticity in the digital age. B. Bots have learned to hold meaningful conversations with people, write poetry, outplay chess grandmasters, draft legal documents, write medical prescriptions, and diagnose how likely they are to have a heart attack. C. AI has also been used to create 'deep fakes' that can turn what we call reality inside out. D. The distinction between Artificial Intelligence (AI) and human intelligence has become increasingly blurred.
questions.explanation: The correct order to form a coherent paragraph is DBCA. Sentence D comes first as it introduces the idea that AI is becoming more advanced, blurring the lines between human intelligence and artificial intelligence. Sentence B comes next as it provides examples of the advanced capabilities of AI. Sentence C then elaborates on one specific use of AI, which is creating deep fakes, highlighting its impact on reality. Sentence A then discusses the ethical and security concerns related to AI-generated content, particularly deep fakes (CA forms a logical pair as both sentences discuss about deep fakes). Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. In some ways, vertical farming is better for the environment because it uses less land, water, and fertilizer than traditional farming. B. However, building the vertical farm and equipping it with a large number of electronics - especially LED lamps for lighting and a very big air conditioning system - requires large amounts of energy, materials, and money, even before the first plant is grown. C. There are several big differences between vertical farming and the traditional type of farming that happens outdoors, in fields. D. Plus, since the plants are grown indoors, there are fewer pests and diseases, so farmers might not need to use as many pesticides or other potentially harmful chemicals to keep the plants healthy.
questions.explanation: Sentence C introduces the overall topic: a comparison between vertical farming and traditional farming. It sets the stage for the rest of the passage by explaining that there are key differences between these two farming methods. This broad opening provides context for the more specific points that follow. After the comparison is introduced in 'C', sentence A begins by explaining one of the advantages of vertical farming. It states how vertical farming is more environmentally friendly than traditional farming by using fewer resources. This directly addresses the differences mentioned in 'C' and continues the discussion by presenting a positive aspect of vertical farming. Sentence D builds on the benefits of vertical farming mentioned in 'A' by adding another positive aspect: reduced use of pesticides. The word "plus" connects it to the previous sentence, continuing the discussion of the environmental advantages of vertical farming. It adds further reasons why vertical farming may be better for the environment. Sentence B introduces a contrast with the earlier points. After highlighting the benefits in 'A' and 'D', sentence B brings up a challenge or drawback of vertical farming: the high cost and energy requirements to set up and operate a vertical farm. The word "however" signals a shift, acknowledging that despite the benefits, vertical farming has significant resource demands. This serves as a conclusion by balancing the positive points with a significant drawback. So, the correct order is CADB. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the proverb underlined in this sentence. Rohit has shifted cities every year, and his uncle warned that a rolling stone gathers no moss.
options: Constant movement prevents stability, security, or growth
Comparing yourself to others drains joy
questions.explanation: The proverb "a rolling stone gathers no moss" means that someone who keeps moving or changing places frequently does not gain stability, security, or long-term growth. In the sentence, Rohit's uncle cautioned him that constantly shifting cities may stop him from settling or progressing. So, the correct answer is: (b) Constant movement prevents stability, security, or growth

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: To paint someone in lurid colours
options: To portray someone as dull or uninteresting
To describe someone in excessively flattering terms
To depict someone in a mysterious or ambiguous manner
To present someone in a shocking, sensational, or exaggeratedly negative way
questions.explanation: "To paint someone in lurid colours" means to describe a person in sensational, exaggeratedly negative, or shocking terms. Option (d) captures this critical, overstated portrayal. Options (a), (b), and (c) are unrelated and do not express the idiom's negative, sensational nature. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the central argument of the passage?
options: Retirement has reduced the productivity of people above fifty in modern societies.
Tourism companies primarily focus on attracting younger travellers rather than older ones.
The increasing tendency of people above fifty to travel is influenced by social, economic, and technological developments that support active aging.
Technological innovations alone are responsible for the rise in global travel among older adults.
questions.explanation: The central argument of the passage is that the growing tendency of people above the age of fifty to travel is the result of several social, economic, and technological changes that allow them to lead more active and independent lives. The passage explains factors such as improved healthcare, longer life expectancy, financial stability, fewer professional responsibilities after retirement, and the development of better tourism infrastructure and technology. These developments together have made travel more convenient and appealing for older adults. This idea is clearly stated in the first paragraph of the passage, where the author explains that changing social and economic conditions have made travel more accessible and attractive for older adults, and it is further supported in the second paragraph, which discusses the role of tourism infrastructure and technology. Therefore, option (c) best captures the central argument of the passage. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: The phrase "this trend" in the sentence "One of the main reasons for this trend is that people above the age of fifty often have fewer work-related responsibilities" most likely refers to which of the following?
options: The growing popularity of senior-specific tourism packages.
The rise in international tourism across all age groups.
The increasing number of individuals over fifty engaging in travel.
The shift of older adults from demanding careers to leisure activities.
questions.explanation: The phrase "this trend" refers to the increase in the number of people traveling after the age of fifty. In the first paragraph of the passage, the author clearly states that there has been a noticeable increase in the number of people traveling after the age of fifty in recent decades. The sentence in the second paragraph then explains the reasons behind "this trend," such as fewer work-related responsibilities and more free time after retirement. Therefore, the phrase points back to the earlier idea about the increasing number of individuals over fifty engaging in travel, is the correct answer. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following best captures the overall tone of the passage?
options: Critical, as it questions the practicality of travel in later life.
Analytical and optimistic, as it explains causes and highlights positive outcomes of the phenomenon.
Nostalgic, as it compares modern travel habits with those of earlier generations.
Sceptical, as it doubts the long-term benefits of travel for older adults.
questions.explanation: The overall tone of the passage is analytical and optimistic. The author systematically explains the reasons behind the increasing trend of travel among people over fifty, such as improved healthcare, longer life expectancy, fewer professional responsibilities, and better tourism infrastructure. At the same time, the passage highlights the positive outcomes of such travel, including physical activity, mental stimulation, social interaction, and improved emotional well-being. This tone of explaining causes while emphasizing benefits is evident throughout the first and third paragraphs of the passage, where both the reasons and the advantages of travel for older adults are discussed. • Critical - This tone would imply the author is questioning or criticizing travel in later life, which the passage does not do; instead, it presents travel positively. • Nostalgic - A nostalgic tone would look back wistfully at the past, comparing it with the present. While the passage mentions that travel was less common earlier, it doesn't express longing or sentimental reflection. • Sceptical - Sceptical would mean the author doubts the benefits or value of travel, but the passage consistently emphasizes its positive effects for older adults. All these are therefore not suitable, leaving analytical and optimistic as the correct tone. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the closest antonym for the word "accessible" as used in the line "changing social and economic conditions have made travel more accessible and attractive for older adults"?
questions.explanation: In the line "changing social and economic conditions have made travel more accessible and attractive for older adults," the word "accessible" means easy to reach, available, or easy to use. The closest antonym would therefore be a word that conveys the opposite idea - something difficult to reach, limited, or not easily available. Among the options, "restricted" best fits this meaning. • Appealing (b) and convenient (c) are similar in meaning, not opposites. • Attainable (d) also aligns with accessibility rather than opposing it. This usage and meaning are discussed in the first paragraph of the passage, where the author explains that changing social and economic conditions have made travel easier for older adults. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the grammatical role of the clause "as many individuals were occupied with professional responsibilities and family commitments" in the sentence "Earlier, travel during later stages of life was relatively uncommon, as many individuals were occupied with professional responsibilities and family commitments"?
options: It functions as an adjective clause modifying the noun "life."
It functions as a conditional clause describing a possible situation.
It functions as a noun clause acting as the object of the verb "was."
It functions as an adverbial clause explaining the reason for the uncommon nature of travel earlier.
questions.explanation: The clause "as many individuals were occupied with professional responsibilities and family commitments" begins with the subordinating conjunction "as" and explains why travel during later stages of life was relatively uncommon. Therefore, it functions as an adverbial clause of reason, modifying the main clause by providing the cause. Adjective clause modifying "life" - Incorrect, because the clause does not describe the noun "life"; it explains a reason, not a quality. Conditional clause describing a possible situation - Incorrect, as there is no hypothetical or "if-then" condition expressed. Noun clause acting as the object of "was" - Incorrect, because the clause does not function as a noun or object; it provides explanatory information. Thus, the correct grammatical role is adverbial clause of reason. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Convert the sentence provided below from its passive voice structure to an active voice structure: A new eco-friendly policy to reduce plastic waste across all departments has been implemented by the management of the multinational corporation.
options: The management of the multinational corporation is implementing a new eco-friendly policy to reduce plastic waste across all departments.
The management of the multinational corporation implements a new eco-friendly policy to reduce plastic waste across all departments.
The management of the multinational corporation has implemented a new eco-friendly policy to reduce plastic waste across all departments.
The management of the multinational corporation had been implementing a new eco-friendly policy to reduce plastic waste across all departments.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Passive Voice is changed into the object of the Active Voice. Also, the object of the Passive Voice is changed into the subject of the Active Voice. The removal of the "to be" verb [been] and the preposition 'by' is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Passive to Active. The Passive voice in this question is in Present Perfect tense. Thus, the Active voice will also be in the Present Perfect. In Passive voice, the format is: Object of the Active voice (Subjective case) + has/have + been + V3 + by + Subject of the Active voice (Objective case). Thus, the format of the Active voice is: Subject + has/have + V3 + Object. For example: A new horse has been bought by Arya. (Passive Voice) Arya has bought a new horse. (Active Voice) The options except (c) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Active Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is option (c): The management of the multinational corporation has implemented a new eco-friendly policy to reduce plastic waste across all departments. Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. LARGESS
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) Munificence. LARGESS (or LARGESSE): This means generosity in giving gifts or money to others; liberal giving. (a) Scarcity: This means a state of being in short supply; a shortage. This is an antonym. (b) Avarice: This means extreme greed for wealth. This is also an antonym. (c) Munificence: This means the quality of being extremely liberal in giving; great generosity. This is a direct synonym for largess. (d) Influence: This means the power to affect someone or something. This is unrelated.

questions.question: Select the option with the correct spellings to replace the underlined words in the given sentence. An operator got the message wrong-whether out of iggnorance or maliece is not clear.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, the correct spellings of the underlined words are 'ignorance' and 'malice'. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. Malice - the desire to harm someone; ill will.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. I am a person who never consumes alcohol, though that does not make me intolerant.
questions.explanation: Anglophile: Someone who greatly admires or is fond of England, its people, culture, or things related to England. Laconic: Using very few words; concise to the point of seeming rude or mysterious. Teetotaller: Someone who abstains completely from alcoholic drinks. Consummate: To bring to completion or perfection; highly skilled or accomplished in a particular field or activity. So, 'teetotaller' is the right choice. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Hard luxury - watches and jewellery - are one of the fastest-growing areas and is the only sector where LVMH (a luxury goods company) is not leader.
options: Hard luxury - watches and jewellery - are
and is the only sector where
LVMH (a luxury goods company) is not leader.
questions.explanation: The error is in part a) of the sentence. The helping verb 'are' needs to be replaced by 'is' as it refers to 'hard luxury' which is a singular noun. So, the correct answer is option a).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. There was an expression of __________ on his face, and he bit the side of his forefinger.
questions.explanation: Condemn - criticise. Contempt - scorn. Conform - adjust. Contend - argue or fight. We need a noun for this blank since it needs to be connected with 'expression' via the preposition 'of', and only B fits here, going by that criterion.
